locator: Declaration of the Rights of Man and Citizen, Article 3, 26 August 1789; English translation at https://avalon.law.yale.edu/18th_century/rightsof.asp
text: The principle of all sovereignty resides essentially in the nation. No body nor individual may exercise any authority which does not proceed directly from the nation.

locator: Robespierre, 'On the Principles of Political Morality,' 5 February 1794; English translation at https://revolution.chnm.org/d/413
text: If the mainspring of popular government in peacetime is virtue, amid revolution it is at the same time [both] virtue and terror: virtue, without which terror is fatal; terror, without which virtue is impotent.
